column1: #   verdict  exit  pct  age_off  reset_off  cred_age  pct_7d  reset7d_off  append  axis  desc
# Offsets are seconds from *now* (static fixtures would age into permanent STALE).
# pct_7d '-' omits the seven_day keys (a three-line snapshot — today's behavior).
# append is the sample-line count the run must leave in the history log (0 or 1);
# axis ('-' / 5h / 7d) asserts which window a PAUSE names. # and blank lines ignored.
# Every verdict branch and both pause axes carry a firing and a non-firing case
# Defaults under test: PAUSE_PCT=80, PAUSE_PCT_7D=95, STALE_AGE=600, LOGIN_WINDOW=600.
# OK — under threshold, fresh reading, live window

column11: integer under threshold
fractional under threshold (non-firing PAUSE)
weekly present but comfortable — neither axis fires
weekly between the split thresholds (80..95) — the looser weekly default does not fire
dead weekly window does not pause (a high 7d pct from a rolled window)
just under the 5h threshold — the at-or-over boundary's non-firing side
just under the weekly threshold — the at-or-over boundary's non-firing side

column1: # PAUSE (5h) — at or over threshold, fresh, live window, no recent login

column11: integer over the 5h threshold
fractional over 5h (the float-compare regression)
fractional just over the 5h boundary (int truncation would miss)
exactly at the 5h threshold pauses (at-or-over: the boundary reading is judged at the limit)
over 5h with an old login (no lag reroute)

column1: # PAUSE (7-day) — the weekly axis fires while 5h sits comfortable, and dominates when both fire

column11: weekly over while 5h comfortable -> PAUSE names the 7-day axis
both axes over -> the costlier weekly axis is named
exactly at the weekly threshold pauses (at-or-over boundary)

column1: # PAUSE — inside the login window the reroute may suppress an OK, never a PAUSE

column11: over 5h with a fresh login -> still PAUSE: a lagging reading must not wave through an expensive dispatch
weekly over with a fresh login -> still PAUSE (the reroute does not reach either axis)

column1: # STALE — post-login lag (the non-blocking outcome only), stale reading, non-numeric fail-closed

column11: under threshold with a fresh login -> STALE: the reroute suppresses the OK, so a lagging pct cannot print a fresh-looking OK
just under the 5h threshold with a fresh login -> STALE: the reroute's firing side at the boundary the PAUSE case pins from above
reading older than STALE_AGE — parsed, so it still logs
non-numeric pct fails closed and appends nothing (not a parsed sample)

column1: # RESET-OK — the 5h window rolled over; the pct is from the dead window

column11: dead 5h window -> RESET-OK (a high pct must not read as PAUSE)
5h window just rolled over
